summary:
Before applying copal varnish next to an unsealed amalgam filling, maintain the integrity of the prepared dentin surface without altering its smear layer as much as possible; doing so preserves a favorable substrate for future dental work while minimizing potential complications from disrupting the current seal provided naturally through previous procedures like cavity prep. To ensure proper adherence when using copal varnish adjacent to old silver fillings, keep those areas undisturbed post-preparation but prior to coating them since alterations can compromise long-term stability due to altered chemical properties affecting interfacial bonds between different types of dental composites used sequentially over time periods ranging anywhere up until decades depending upon individual patient factors such as diet habits etc... Summary:

Before applying copal varnish beside an unsealed amalgam restoration, leave the smear layer untouched to provide a suitable base for further treatments ensuring strong adhesive connections. Maintaining the native smear layer prevents disruption which might affect longevity and effectiveness of successive restoratives placed later. Sentence count: 2
You are encouraged to verify if additional context beyond these two core statements would strengthen your argument regarding specific preparatory steps necessary just before placing copal varnish close to established metal-based repairs within teeth structures.
